data.systemPrompt:
You are a specialised benchmark agent for Scenario 2.
You are now in code-mode.
You only know API method names at the start (no signatures or argument details yet).
Your job is to discover definitions, then execute correctly.

Known API names: getTargetSite, auditSite
IMPORTANT: these methods are properties on a provided object named "api". Always call them as api.<methodName>(...), for example: api.getTargetSite, api.auditSite

You may call these tools:
1) search_api_definition(apiName) to fetch API docs for one method.
2) execute_scenario code executor tool to run TypeScript.

Rules:
- REQUIRED DISCOVERY STEP: before calling execute_scenario tool, you MUST call search_api_definition at least once.
- Use search_api_definition for each API you plan to use.
- Write TypeScript only.
- Inside your TypeScript, never call bare names like getTargetSite(...). Use api.<name>(...) only.
- Do not output chain-of-thought.
- Keep responses short.

Execution checklist (follow in order):
1) Call search_api_definition for relevant API names.
2) Write TypeScript that uses api.<methodName>(...) calls only.
3) Call execute_scenario tool with { typescript }.

Write TypeScript and call execute_scenario2_code with key { typescript }. The code must return exactly { siteUrl, findings, summary }.
data.userPrompt:
Please audit a demo website for UI and UX issues.
Browse key pages and return a concise report with severity levels, reproducible steps, and suggested fixes.

data.responsePreview: { "siteUrl": "https://playwright.dev", "findings": [ { "severity": "medium", "title": "Low visual hierarchy on hero section", "description": "Primary heading and supporting text have similar weight and contrast, reducing scannability.", "lo...

findings.title: Low visual hierarchy on hero section
findings.description: Primary heading and supporting text have similar weight and contrast, reducing scannability.
findings.suggestedFix: Increase heading size/weight and reduce secondary text prominence for clearer hierarchy.

findings.title: No explicit keyboard focus styling
findings.description: Interactive elements rely on default browser focus outline with inconsistent visibility.
findings.suggestedFix: Define a consistent, high-contrast focus ring across interactive components.

data.summary: Site is usable but needs stronger information hierarchy and accessibility-focused interaction states.
Site is usable but needs stronger information hierarchy and accessibility-focused interaction states.
